name: Brihaspati Extended
description: Brihaspati, also known as Guru, is the deity representing wisdom, eloquence, and religious merit in Hindu mythology. He is the preceptor of the gods (Devaguru), a master of Dharma, and the chief priest. Born from the primordial sound 'Om' or sometimes attributed to the sage Angiras, Brihaspati embodies knowledge and divine grace. He is often depicted as a learned scholar, radiating golden light, symbolizing his wisdom and auspicious nature. As the lord of Jupiter (Guru Graha), Brihaspati influences fortune, prosperity, and spiritual growth. His mantra is chanted to seek guidance, wisdom, and success in all endeavors. Brihaspati's influence extends to education, law, and justice, making him a pivotal figure in maintaining cosmic order. He is revered for his ability to resolve disputes and guide individuals towards righteous paths. Stories often depict him mediating between gods and demons, demonstrating his diplomatic skills and wisdom. He is married to Tara, who was abducted by Chandra (the Moon), leading to a cosmic war. Their son, Budha (Mercury), is a testament to the complex relationships within the Hindu pantheon. Brihaspati's teachings are considered invaluable, offering guidance on matters of spirituality, ethics, and societal harmony. He is the embodiment of divine intellect and serves as a beacon of knowledge for all beings. His blessings are sought for success in education, career, and spiritual pursuits, making him a central figure in Vedic astrology and Hindu worship.
subtitle: Preceptor of the Gods; Divine Wisdom

keyMyths.title: Birth of Brihaspati
keyMyths.description: Brihaspati is believed to have originated from the primordial sound 'Om' or from the sage Angiras, marking him as the embodiment of divine knowledge and wisdom.

keyMyths.title: Tara's Abduction
keyMyths.description: Chandra, the Moon god, abducted Tara, Brihaspati's wife, leading to a cosmic war between the gods and demons, showcasing Brihaspati's strength and influence.

keyMyths.title: Brihaspati's Teachings
keyMyths.description: Brihaspati's teachings are highly valued, offering guidance on spirituality, ethics, and societal harmony, making him a central figure in Hindu philosophical discourse.
